# cloudfly_instance (Resource)

Manages a CloudFly compute instance.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "cloudfly_instance" "example" {
  name        = "example-instance"
  region      = "HN-Cloud01"
  flavor_type = "Standard"
  image_name  = "CentOS-7.9"
  ram         = 1
  vcpus       = 1
  disk        = 20
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `disk` (Number)
- `flavor_type` (String)
- `image_name` (String)
- `ram` (Number)
- `region` (String)
- `vcpus` (Number)

### Optional

- `admin_password` (String, Sensitive) New administrator password for the instance.
- `auto_backup` (Boolean)
- `enable_ipv6` (Boolean)
- `enable_private_network` (Boolean)
- `name` (String)
- `power_state` (String) Desired power state of the instance. Valid values: `running`, `stopped`.
- `reboot` (Boolean) Set to `true` to reboot the instance. Resets to `null` after the reboot completes.
- `reverse_dns` (String) Reverse DNS entry for the instance's primary IPv4 address.
- `security_group_ids` (List of String) Security group IDs to assign to the instance.
- `ssh_key_ids` (List of Number)

### Read-Only

- `access_ipv4` (String)
- `access_ipv6` (String)
- `backup_server` (String)
- `created` (String)
- `current_month_traffic` (String)
- `current_month_traffic_mb` (String)
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `remain_max_ip_addon` (String)
- `status` (String)
- `stopped_by_cloudfly` (Boolean)
- `task_state` (String)
- `username` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import cloudfly_instance.example "<instance-id>"
```
